prevalence.trend: stable to increasing with aging and diabetes prevalence

incidence.value: 1-3% per year among people with diabetes

vulnerablePopulations: patients with long duration of diabetes
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical exam with monofilament/vibration testing and symptom assessment; guideline-based annual foot screening common

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: moderate to high; annual foot screening common in many systems
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate to high with structured diabetes care and foot clinics; varies by member state

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Integrated diabetes complication screening pathways and multidisciplinary foot clinics in many countries
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Risk-factor control, foot care and ulcer prevention, neuropathic pain treatment (duloxetine/pregabalin), and rapid ulcer referral pathways
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Care variability across member states and persistent ulcer burden in high-risk groups
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Structured diabetes care systems and access to multidisciplinary foot services in many member states
